description: Required public API functions are importable and callable.
test_code:
from minidotenv import dotenv_values, load_dotenv, find_dotenv, get_key, set_key, unset_key
print(all(callable(x) for x in [dotenv_values, load_dotenv, find_dotenv, get_key, set_key, unset_key]))

description: dotenv_path takes precedence over stream input.
test_code:
import io, tempfile, os
from minidotenv import dotenv_values
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    path = os.path.join(td, '.env')
    open(path, 'w', encoding='utf-8').write('A=file\n')
    vals = dotenv_values(path, stream=io.StringIO('A=stream\n'))
    print(vals)

description: Basic KEY=value, empty value, and bare key parse to string, empty string, and None.
test_code:
import io
from minidotenv import dotenv_values
vals = dotenv_values(stream=io.StringIO('A=1\nEMPTY=\nBARE\n'))
print(vals)

description: Comments, blank lines, export prefixes, and inline comments are handled.
test_code:
import io
from minidotenv import dotenv_values
content = '# top

export A=1
B=two # trailing
C=hash#literal
'
print(dotenv_values(stream=io.StringIO(content)))

description: Single and double quoted values remove quotes and decode supported escapes.
test_code:
import io
from minidotenv import dotenv_values
content = "S='one two'\nD=\"line\\nnext\"\nT=\"tab\\tend\"\n"
vals = dotenv_values(stream=io.StringIO(content))
print(vals['S'])
print(repr(vals['D']))
print(repr(vals['T']))

description: Interpolation supports earlier file variables and default values.
test_code:
import io, os
from minidotenv import dotenv_values
os.environ.pop('BASE', None)
content = 'BASE=/app\nLOG=${BASE}/log\nMISS=${UNSET:-fallback}\n'
print(dotenv_values(stream=io.StringIO(content)))

description: Interpolation does not see bindings defined later in the file.
test_code:
import io, os
from minidotenv import dotenv_values
os.environ.pop('LATER', None)
content = 'FIRST=${LATER}x\nLATER=value\n'
print(dotenv_values(stream=io.StringIO(content)))

description: load_dotenv respects override mode and skips None values.
test_code:
import io, os
from minidotenv import load_dotenv
os.environ['A'] = 'env'
os.environ.pop('BARE', None)
load_dotenv(stream=io.StringIO('A=file\nBARE\n'), override=False)
print(os.environ.get('A'))
print('BARE' in os.environ)
load_dotenv(stream=io.StringIO('A=file\n'), override=True)
print(os.environ.get('A'))

description: find_dotenv searches upward from the current working directory.
test_code:
import os, tempfile
from minidotenv import find_dotenv
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    os.makedirs(os.path.join(td, 'a', 'b'))
    open(os.path.join(td, '.env'), 'w', encoding='utf-8').write('A=1\n')
    old = os.getcwd()
    os.chdir(os.path.join(td, 'a', 'b'))
    try:
        found = find_dotenv()
        print(os.path.basename(found))
        print(os.path.dirname(found) == td)
    finally:
        os.chdir(old)

description: set_key, get_key, and unset_key operate on one file deterministically.
test_code:
import os, tempfile
from minidotenv import set_key, get_key, unset_key, dotenv_values
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    path = os.path.join(td, '.env')
    open(path, 'w', encoding='utf-8').write('A=1\n')
    print(set_key(path, 'B', 'two', quote_mode='never'))
    print(get_key(path, 'B'))
    print(unset_key(path, 'A'))
    print(dotenv_values(path))

description: set_key replaces the first matching key without duplicating it.
test_code:
import os, tempfile
from minidotenv import set_key, dotenv_values
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    path = os.path.join(td, '.env')
    open(path, 'w', encoding='utf-8').write('A=old\nB=keep\n')
    set_key(path, 'A', 'new', quote_mode='never')
    text = open(path, encoding='utf-8').read()
    print(dotenv_values(path))
    print(text.count('A=') == 1)

description: Malformed lines and invalid keys are skipped while later valid bindings parse.
test_code:
import io
from minidotenv import dotenv_values
content = 'GOOD=1\n1BAD=no\nnot a binding ?\nNEXT=2\n'
print(dotenv_values(stream=io.StringIO(content)))

description: Parsed values feed interpolation and then environment loading.
test_code:
import io, os
from minidotenv import load_dotenv
os.environ.pop('ROOT', None); os.environ.pop('CONF', None)
content = 'ROOT=/srv\nCONF=${ROOT}/conf\n'
load_dotenv(stream=io.StringIO(content), override=True)
print(os.environ.get('CONF'))

description: File mutation affects later parsing, loading, and key lookup.
test_code:
import os, tempfile
from minidotenv import set_key, get_key, load_dotenv
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    path = os.path.join(td, '.env')
    open(path, 'w', encoding='utf-8').write('A=1\n')
    set_key(path, 'B', '2', quote_mode='never')
    os.environ.pop('B', None)
    load_dotenv(path, override=True)
    print(get_key(path, 'B'))
    print(os.environ.get('B'))

description: dotenv_values is read-only while load_dotenv mutates os.environ.
test_code:
import io, os
from minidotenv import dotenv_values, load_dotenv
os.environ.pop('ONLY_PARSE', None)
vals = dotenv_values(stream=io.StringIO('ONLY_PARSE=1\n'))
print(vals)
print('ONLY_PARSE' in os.environ)
load_dotenv(stream=io.StringIO('ONLY_PARSE=2\n'), override=True)
print(os.environ.get('ONLY_PARSE'))

description: A failed set_key with an invalid key does not corrupt the original file.
test_code:
import os, tempfile
from minidotenv import set_key, dotenv_values
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    path = os.path.join(td, '.env')
    open(path, 'w', encoding='utf-8').write('GOOD=1\n# keep\n')
    before = open(path, encoding='utf-8').read()
    try:
        result = set_key(path, '1BAD', 'x', quote_mode='never')
    except Exception:
        result = False
    after = open(path, encoding='utf-8').read()
    print(result)
    print(before == after)
    print(dotenv_values(path))

description: Override mode changes interpolation and final loaded environment values.
test_code:
import io, os
from minidotenv import dotenv_values, load_dotenv
os.environ['BASE'] = 'env'
content = 'BASE=file\nPATH=${BASE}/bin\n'
print(dotenv_values(stream=io.StringIO(content), override=False))
print(dotenv_values(stream=io.StringIO(content), override=True))
os.environ['PATH_OUT'] = 'old'
load_dotenv(stream=io.StringIO('PATH_OUT=${BASE}/out\n'), override=True)
print(os.environ['PATH_OUT'])

description: Path discovery, environment priority, and interpolation compose from a nested cwd.
test_code:
import os, tempfile
from minidotenv import find_dotenv, load_dotenv
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    os.makedirs(os.path.join(td, 'child', 'deep'))
    open(os.path.join(td, '.env'), 'w', encoding='utf-8').write('BASE=file\nOUT=${BASE}/x\n')
    os.environ['BASE'] = 'env'
    os.environ.pop('OUT', None)
    old = os.getcwd(); os.chdir(os.path.join(td, 'child', 'deep'))
    try:
        found = find_dotenv()
        load_dotenv(found, override=False)
        print(os.path.dirname(found) == td)
        print(os.environ.get('BASE'))
        print(os.environ.get('OUT'))
    finally:
        os.chdir(old)

description: unset_key persists across later reads while preserving unrelated lines and comments.
test_code:
import os, tempfile
from minidotenv import unset_key, get_key, dotenv_values
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    path = os.path.join(td, '.env')
    open(path, 'w', encoding='utf-8').write('# keep\nA=1\nB=2\n')
    print(unset_key(path, 'A'))
    print(get_key(path, 'A'))
    print(dotenv_values(path))
    print('# keep' in open(path, encoding='utf-8').read())

description: Malformed lines are skipped and do not prevent later mutation and parsing.
test_code:
import os, tempfile
from minidotenv import dotenv_values, set_key
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    path = os.path.join(td, '.env')
    open(path, 'w', encoding='utf-8').write('A=1\nbad line ?\nB=2\n')
    print(dotenv_values(path))
    set_key(path, 'C', '3', quote_mode='never')
    print(dotenv_values(path))

description: Replacing a key changes later interpolation results without duplicating state.
test_code:
import os, tempfile
from minidotenv import set_key, dotenv_values
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    path = os.path.join(td, '.env')
    open(path, 'w', encoding='utf-8').write('ROOT=old\nOUT=${ROOT}/out\n')
    print(dotenv_values(path)['OUT'])
    set_key(path, 'ROOT', 'new', quote_mode='never')
    vals = dotenv_values(path)
    print(vals['OUT'])
    print(open(path, encoding='utf-8').read().count('ROOT=') == 1)

description: Missing files are handled safely across read, load, get, and unset helpers.
test_code:
import os, tempfile
from minidotenv import dotenv_values, load_dotenv, get_key, unset_key
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    path = os.path.join(td, 'missing.env')
    print(dotenv_values(path))
    print(load_dotenv(path))
    print(get_key(path, 'A'))
    print(unset_key(path, 'A'))

description: Quotes, comments, and interpolation interact without losing literal content.
test_code:
import io
from minidotenv import dotenv_values
content = "A='hash # kept'\nB=two # cut\nC=\"${B} value\"\nD=${A}\n"
print(dotenv_values(stream=io.StringIO(content)))

description: A mutated file takes precedence over a conflicting stream in later reads.
test_code:
import io, os, tempfile
from minidotenv import set_key, dotenv_values
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    path = os.path.join(td, '.env')
    open(path, 'w', encoding='utf-8').write('A=old\n')
    set_key(path, 'A', 'file', quote_mode='never')
    vals = dotenv_values(path, stream=io.StringIO('A=stream\n'))
    print(vals)

description: Implicit discovery is used without leaking dotenv_values state into os.environ or later directories.
test_code:
import os, tempfile
from minidotenv import dotenv_values, load_dotenv, find_dotenv
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    root = os.path.join(td, 'root')
    other = os.path.join(td, 'other')
    os.makedirs(os.path.join(root, 'a', 'b'))
    os.makedirs(other)
    open(os.path.join(root, '.env'), 'w', encoding='utf-8').write('IMPLICIT=one\nCOMBINED=${IMPLICIT}/x\n')
    os.environ.pop('IMPLICIT', None); os.environ.pop('COMBINED', None)
    old = os.getcwd()
    try:
        os.chdir(os.path.join(root, 'a', 'b'))
        print(os.path.basename(find_dotenv()))
        print(dotenv_values())
        print('IMPLICIT' in os.environ)
        print(load_dotenv(override=True))
        print(os.environ.get('COMBINED'))
        os.chdir(other)
        print(find_dotenv())
        print(dotenv_values())
    finally:
        os.chdir(old)

description: Multiple mutations replace export bindings, append new keys, remove only the first duplicate, and affect later load results.
test_code:
import os, tempfile
from minidotenv import set_key, unset_key, dotenv_values, load_dotenv
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    path = os.path.join(td, '.env')
    open(path, 'w', encoding='utf-8').write('# top\nexport A=old\nB=first\nB=second\n')
    print(set_key(path, 'A', 'new', quote_mode='never'))
    print(set_key(path, 'C', '${A}/c', quote_mode='never'))
    print(unset_key(path, 'B'))
    vals = dotenv_values(path)
    print(vals)
    os.environ.pop('A', None); os.environ.pop('B', None); os.environ.pop('C', None)
    load_dotenv(path, override=True)
    print(os.environ.get('A'), os.environ.get('B'), os.environ.get('C'))
    text = open(path, encoding='utf-8').read()
    print('# top' in text)
    print(text.count('B=') == 1)
expected_output:
True
True
True
{'A': 'new', 'B': 'second', 'C': 'new/c'}
new second new/c
True
True

description: Failed mutations and malformed lines preserve the file and do not prevent later valid mutation.
test_code:
import os, tempfile
from minidotenv import set_key, unset_key, dotenv_values
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    missing = os.path.join(td, 'missing.env')
    path = os.path.join(td, '.env')
    open(path, 'w', encoding='utf-8').write('A=1\nmalformed line ?\nB=2\n')
    before = open(path, encoding='utf-8').read()
    print(set_key(missing, 'X', '1', quote_mode='never'))
    print(set_key(path, 'BAD-KEY', 'x', quote_mode='never'))
    print(unset_key(missing, 'A'))
    print(open(path, encoding='utf-8').read() == before)
    print(set_key(path, 'C', '3', quote_mode='never'))
    print(unset_key(path, 'A'))
    print(dotenv_values(path))
expected_output:
False
False
False
True
True
True
{'B': '2', 'C': '3'}

description: interpolate=False keeps literal placeholders through parse and load while later calls still interpolate normally.
test_code:
import io, os
from minidotenv import dotenv_values, load_dotenv
os.environ['BASE'] = 'envbase'
os.environ.pop('OUT', None); os.environ.pop('RAW', None)
content = 'BASE=filebase\nOUT=${BASE}/out\nRAW=${MISSING:-fallback}\n'
print(dotenv_values(stream=io.StringIO(content), interpolate=False))
load_dotenv(stream=io.StringIO(content), override=True, interpolate=False)
print(os.environ.get('OUT'))
print(os.environ.get('RAW'))
print(dotenv_values(stream=io.StringIO(content), interpolate=True, override=True)['OUT'])

description: quote_mode=always writes escaped single-quoted content that survives get, parse, and unrelated comment preservation.
test_code:
import os, tempfile
from minidotenv import set_key, get_key, dotenv_values
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    path = os.path.join(td, '.env')
    open(path, 'w', encoding='utf-8').write('# keep\nA=1\n')
    value = "two words 'quoted' \\ slash"
    print(set_key(path, 'COMPLEX', value, quote_mode='always'))
    print(get_key(path, 'COMPLEX'))
    print(dotenv_values(path)['COMPLEX'] == value)
    text = open(path, encoding='utf-8').read()
    print('# keep' in text)
    print("COMPLEX='" in text)
expected_output:
True
two words 'quoted' \ slash
True
True
True

description: Repeated stream and file parses do not leak earlier bindings into later sources.
test_code:
import io, os, tempfile
from minidotenv import dotenv_values
os.environ.pop('LOCAL', None)
first = dotenv_values(stream=io.StringIO('LOCAL=first\nOUT=${LOCAL}/one\n'))
second = dotenv_values(stream=io.StringIO('OUT=${LOCAL:-fallback}/two\nLOCAL=second\n'))
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    path = os.path.join(td, '.env')
    open(path, 'w', encoding='utf-8').write('OUT=${LOCAL:-filefallback}/three\n')
    third = dotenv_values(path)
print(first)
print(second)
print(third)

description: The export prefix accepts whitespace before a binding and the parsed value feeds later interpolation and loading.
test_code:
import io, os
from minidotenv import dotenv_values, load_dotenv
os.environ.pop('A', None); os.environ.pop('B', None)
content = 'export\tA=1\nB=${A}/b\n'
print(dotenv_values(stream=io.StringIO(content)))
print(load_dotenv(stream=io.StringIO(content), override=True))
print(os.environ.get('B'))

description: Appending a new key to a file without a trailing newline creates a separate binding that later parsing can read.
test_code:
import os, tempfile
from minidotenv import set_key, dotenv_values
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    path = os.path.join(td, '.env')
    open(path, 'w', encoding='utf-8').write('A=1')
    print(set_key(path, 'B', '2', quote_mode='never'))
    print(repr(open(path, encoding='utf-8').read()))
    print(dotenv_values(path))

description: Appending after a final comment without a trailing newline preserves the comment and creates a parseable binding.
test_code:
import os, tempfile
from minidotenv import set_key, dotenv_values
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    path = os.path.join(td, '.env')
    open(path, 'w', encoding='utf-8').write('# keep')
    print(set_key(path, 'A', '1', quote_mode='never'))
    text = open(path, encoding='utf-8').read()
    print(repr(text))
    print(dotenv_values(path))
    print('# keep' in text)

description: A quoted append to a file without a trailing newline survives parsing and environment loading.
test_code:
import os, tempfile
from minidotenv import set_key, dotenv_values, load_dotenv
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    path = os.path.join(td, '.env')
    open(path, 'w', encoding='utf-8').write('A=1')
    os.environ.pop('B', None)
    print(set_key(path, 'B', 'two words', quote_mode='always'))
    print(dotenv_values(path))
    print(load_dotenv(path, override=True))
    print(os.environ.get('B'))
